title: Fix refresh tokens that never expire, rotate, or validate
description:
# Issue #90 - Stolen refresh tokens work forever

Security review of our auth flow found that POST /api/auth/refresh hands out new access tokens for ANY string, never expires refresh tokens, and never rotates them - a stolen token grants access forever.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- refresh tokens unknown to the server are rejected with 401
- a successful refresh ROTATES the token: it returns a new refresh_token and the old one stops working
- refresh tokens older than REFRESH_TTL seconds are rejected with 401 (keep using the module-level REFRESH_TTL constant)
optimal_hint: Ideal: look the token up in REFRESH_TOKENS (401 if absent), check time.time() - entry['created_at'] > REFRESH_TTL reading REFRESH_TTL at call time (401 + delete if expired), then pop the old token and issue/store a fresh one. Watch for solutions that validate but forget to delete the old token (no rotation).
